_comment: Registry for the GitHub Projects v2 boards provisioner (provision_projects.py). One entry per board; 'key' is the CLI selector, 'title' is the exact board title on GitHub (adopt-by-title: an existing board with this title is ADOPTED, never re-created; alternatively pin 'existing_node_id'). 'repos' are linked to the board (issues can be added from any repo regardless — linking only aids the UI pickers). 'standard_fields' are created per board when missing (single-select custom fields). The BUILT-IN Status field cannot be reliably reshaped via the API: 'status_options' is the TARGET the check mode diffs against and reports; reconciling Status options is a one-time manual UI step (see docs/guides/PROJECTS_BOARDS.md). 'collaborators' are best-effort invites (user-owned project support varies — manual UI invite is the documented fallback). See ADR D-010 / docs/design/reports-ecosystem.md.
_repo_field_comment: No custom 'Repo'/'Repository' field is created: those names are RESERVED by GitHub Projects v2 (it has a BUILT-IN Repository field that auto-populates from each item's issue/PR — group/filter board views by that instead). createProjectV2Field rejects them with 'Name cannot have a reserved value' (caught live 2026-07-12).
_excluded_notes: Board node ids were harvested from the first execute (2026-07-12) and pinned in existing_node_id, so re-runs ADOPT these exact boards (no adopt-by-title duplicate risk if a title is edited). The Fitness board is the one wired into fitness-app-backend's GH_REPORTS_*PROJECT_ID env; if a different pre-existing Fitness board should be canonical, replace fitness.existing_node_id with its id before the next run.
